name: MCP Config
what_it_does: MCP configuration management tool that helps developers manage and configure multiple MCP servers across different AI clients. Provides a unified interface for adding, removing, and managing MCP server configurations in Claude Desktop, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible clients without manually editing JSON config files.
use_cases: Managing MCP server configurations across multiple AI clients from a unified CLI
Adding and removing MCP servers without manual JSON config editing
Synchronizing MCP configurations across development environments
Discovering and installing MCP servers with automated configuration
not_for: Running MCP server logic (this is a config management tool, not an MCP server itself)
Production server deployments requiring persistent MCP configs
Teams using single AI client with manual config management
best_when: A developer managing multiple MCP servers across multiple AI clients wants a CLI tool to simplify configuration management — avoiding manual JSON editing across multiple config files.
avoid_when: You only have 1-2 MCP servers configured. The tool adds value when managing complexity across multiple clients and servers.

auth.notes: No authentication — local config management tool. Modifies local AI client config files.

pricing.notes: Free open source CLI tool by marcusschiesser.

known_agent_gotchas: Configuration management tool — not itself an MCP server providing tools to agents
Modifies AI client config files — backup configs before use
Client compatibility varies — verify support for your specific AI client version
Community tool with moderate adoption — test with your specific client before relying on it
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local config file management. No external access. Modifies client config files that may contain API keys for other MCP servers — handle with care.

security_score_components.security_notes: Config file management tool. May read/write config files containing API keys. Local only.
